home.reviewsDesc: What the community is saying about Nioh 3
home.review1: This is the most approachable Nioh; controls feel insanely smooth, and it might be the best action game since Devil May Cry 5.
home.review2: Exploration is one of the best since Elden Ring: a mix of linear missions + open-field + Metroidvania-style backtracking.
home.review3: The game is great but optimization is terrible; even on a high-end PC I need dynamic resolution to lock 120 fps.
home.review4: I played both on PS5 Pro and PC (3080 Ti) and both feel great, very enjoyable.
home.review5: After the rough tutorial and the first boss, controls feel insanely smooth - this might be the best action game since DMC5.
home.review6: My PC (3080 + i5-12600K) had zero issues even in the open area—no crashes, no stutters, no frame drops.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.a1: Nioh 3 is a dark fantasy action RPG set in Japan's Sengoku period, developed by Team NINJA and published by Koei Tecmo Games. The game combines brutal souls-like combat with deep RPG mechanics, featuring a revolutionary dual combat style system (Samurai vs Ninja) and open-field exploration. Nioh 3 releases February 6, 2026 on PlayStation 5 and PC (Steam).
faq.q2: When is the Nioh 3 release date?
faq.a2: Nioh 3 officially launches on February 6, 2026 for PlayStation 5 and PC (Steam). The demo is already available with progress transfer to the full game, and players who complete the demo receive the exclusive Twin-Snake Helmet reward.
faq.q3: What platforms is Nioh 3 available on?
faq.a3: Nioh 3 releases on PS5 and PC (Steam). Both platforms support up to 3-player co-op multiplayer. PS5 Pro offers enhanced graphics with performance modes up to 120fps, while standard PS5 runs at 1440p/60fps or 1080p/120fps.
faq.q4: What is the difference between Samurai Style and Ninja Style in Nioh 3?
faq.a4: Samurai Style focuses on frontal combat with heavy attacks, high poise, and defensive stances—ideal for calculated, methodical players. Ninja Style emphasizes speed, agility, aerial combos, and evasive maneuvers—perfect for aggressive, mobile playstyles. You can switch between styles mid-combat in Nioh 3 for tactical advantage.
faq.q5: Can I transfer my Nioh 3 demo save to the full game?
faq.a5: Yes! All progress from the Nioh 3 demo transfers to the full game when it launches on February 6, 2026. Complete the demo to unlock the exclusive Twin-Snake Helmet reward that carries over to your main save file.
faq.a6: Nioh 3 features Open Field exploration—a hybrid of linear story missions and open-world areas. You'll explore interconnected regions with Metroidvania-style backtracking and hidden paths, not a fully open world like Elden Ring. This design maintains Nioh's focused combat while adding exploration depth.
faq.q7: What is the Crucible mode in Nioh 3?
faq.a7: The Crucible is Nioh 3's core endgame challenge system—a gauntlet of increasingly difficult encounters that test your mastery of combat mechanics, build optimization, and strategy. It's similar to previous games' Abyss or Underworld modes, offering exclusive rewards and the ultimate test of skill.
faq.q8: How many players can play Nioh 3 co-op?
faq.a8: Nioh 3 supports up to 3 players in co-op multiplayer mode. Team up with friends to tackle bosses, explore open fields, and conquer the Crucible together. Co-op is available on both PS5 and PC (Steam) platforms.
faq.q9: What are the best weapons in Nioh 3?
faq.a9: Top-tier weapons in Nioh 3 include Katana (balanced damage and speed), Dual Swords (high DPS and mobility), Odachi (heavy damage and range), and Kusarigama (versatile range and crowd control). Check our weapons tier list for detailed rankings and build recommendations.
faq.q10: Is Nioh 3 harder than Elden Ring?
faq.a10: Nioh 3 emphasizes technical combat with stance management, ki pulse timing, and build optimization—making it mechanically deeper than Elden Ring. Boss difficulty is comparable, but Nioh 3 rewards mastery of its complex systems more than Elden Ring's exploration-focused design. Both games are challenging in different ways.
